workload_pure_qa: 8 factual Qs, pure multi-turn Q&A (no tool-role, system+user only)
verdict: NO task-success degradation (7/8 == 7/8, byte-identical outputs, delta=0). Mimir's mimir-policy scheduling + auto-reclaim preserve answer quality.
note: The one shared error (17*23 -> 401 not 391) is the model's arithmetic limitation, identical both sides — not a Mimir regression. This validates the 'task_success_rate ~flat' scoring claim with a high baseline (87.5%).
